mission.title: microcks: Enable Google PubSub Emulator
mission.description: Enable Google PubSub Emulator. Community-requested feature.

steps.title: Check current microcks deployment
steps.description:
Verify your Microcks deployment:
```bash
# If deployed via Helm on Kubernetes
kubectl get pods -n microcks
helm list -n microcks 2>/dev/null || echo "Not installed via Helm"

# If deployed via Docker Compose
docker compose ps
```
This feature requires Microcks 1.10.0+ which adds Google PubSub Emulator support.

steps.title: Review microcks configuration
steps.description:
Review the Microcks configuration for PubSub settings:
```bash
# Check Helm values for async features
helm get values microcks -n microcks 2>/dev/null

# Or check Docker Compose configuration
cat docker-compose.yml | grep -A5 PUBSUB
```
The Google PubSub Emulator support requires two environment variables: `PUBSUB_EMULATOR_HOST` and `PUBSUB_PROJECT_ID` to configure a client to use the emulator instead of the real PubSub service.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Enable Google PubSub Emulator
steps.description:
The fix enables Google PubSub Emulator support in Microcks:

- Set `PUBSUB_EMULATOR_HOST` on the PubSub producer to configure it for an emulator
- Use a host name in the `endpointUrl` spec for the PubSub consumer to consume from an emulator

Example Helm values:
```yaml
async:
  googlepubsub:
    emulator:
      enabled: true
      host: "pubsub-emulator:8085"
      project: "my-test-project"
```

See the fix PR for details: https://github.com/microcks/microcks/pull/1851

steps.title: Verify the feature works
steps.description:
Verify the PubSub Emulator integration works:
```bash
# Check Microcks async minion logs for PubSub connections
kubectl logs -l app=microcks-async-minion -n microcks --tail=50

# Verify the emulator environment variables are set
kubectl get pods -n microcks -l app=microcks -o jsonpath='{.items[0].spec.containers[0].env}' | jq .
```
Confirm that Microcks can produce and consume messages via the PubSub Emulator.

resolution.summary:
- Use `PUBSUB_EMULATOR_HOST` on the PubSub producer to configure for an emulator
- Use a host name in the endpointUrl spec for the PubSub consumer to consume from an emulator

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with microcks installed or the issue environment reproducible.
